layman_headline: BONK is deeply oversold after a 30% crash — a cautious bounce trade in a fearful market.
layman_explanation: The meme coin BONK has dropped nearly 30% in just five days and its RSI reading of 29 says it's oversold — historically a zone where short-term bounces happen. The broader crypto market is in 'Fear' mode at 33/100, but that reading is actually improving from 25 yesterday, suggesting the worst of the panic may be fading. Bitcoin is holding steady near $66K with ETF inflows continuing for six straight days, which provides a floor for the overall market. This is a high-risk, high-reward mean-reversion play on a beaten-down asset.
layman_action: This is a speculative bounce trade only suitable for risk-tolerant investors. If you enter, use a tight stop-loss at $0.00000255 (about 16% downside) and target a 20% bounce to $0.00000365. Keep the position small.
expert_reasoning: BONK's RSI at 29.14 is the only genuinely oversold reading on the candidate list, and our signal credibility data shows RSI as the highest-conviction indicator (83% win rate, +6.1% avg return). The 19.4% weekly and 29.6% five-day drawdowns suggest capitulation selling that often precedes at least a technical relief bounce. The bearish chart structure (-0.7 bias) is a clear concern — price is below all major MAs — but in mean-reversion trades, that's expected at the entry point. BTC dominance at 56.8% is a headwind for alts, but the improving Fear & Greed trend (25→33) and six consecutive days of Bitcoin ETF inflows ($203M latest) suggest the broader market is stabilizing. I'm setting a conservative target of +20% with a 16% stop-loss, giving roughly 1.25:1 reward/risk — acceptable for a high-probability RSI bounce but reflecting the challenging alt environment.
risk_factors: High BTC dominance (56.8%) actively suppressing altcoin performance — BONK could continue bleeding despite being oversold
Bearish chart structure with price below all major moving averages means any bounce could be a dead cat bounce
Meme coins have no fundamental floor — BONK is 94.8% off ATH and could easily make new lows in a risk-off environment
watch_list: BTC price action around $66K — a breakdown below $65K would invalidate bounce setups across altcoins
Fear & Greed index continuation — watch if it keeps improving above 35 or reverses back toward 25
BONK daily RSI — if it drops below 25 without bouncing, the oversold signal is failing
market_context: Bitcoin is consolidating near $66K with sustained ETF inflows ($203M latest day), but BTC dominance at 56.8% signals capital rotating away from alts. The Fear & Greed index is improving (25→33) suggesting the market may be finding a short-term floor.
context_banner: Oversold BONK offers a disciplined mean-reversion bounce trade as market fear begins to ease and Bitcoin holds steady with ETF inflows.

key_signals.description: BONK RSI at 29.14 is deeply oversold — our most reliable signal (83% win rate) historically leading to short-term bounces.
Fear & Greed improving from 25 to 33 with an upward trend, suggesting panic selling is subsiding — our second most reliable signal (67% win rate).
Bitcoin ETFs extended their inflow streak to six days with $203M added, providing macro support for the broader crypto market.
BONK bearish chart bias (-0.7) with price below all MAs confirms this is a counter-trend bounce trade requiring strict risk management.
Bitcoin holding near $66,300 as risk assets rally provides a supportive floor, though the Balance stablecoin exploit adds general caution.
